content.claim: Once assessed, children frequently do not receive the legally prescribed habilitation and therapy hours specified in expert opinions, indicating a second-stage implementation failure distinct from diagnosis.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: This claim is based on direct practitioner testimony rather than a quantitative audit of prescribed vs. delivered service hours
No systematic count of schools or regions with prescribed-service delivery shortfalls was located
Root cause attribution to staff shortages vs. other factors relies on practitioner inference
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
